section_or_category: penalties (separate add-on, not part of 0-18 index)
scoring_rule: The states were scored 1 to 4, with 1 indicating the weakest penalties and 4 the strictest.
source_quote: States that speciﬁed larger ﬁnes or harsher prison sentences for violations were considered to have stricter regulations than those with smaller or no ﬁnes papers/text/Newmark_2005__state_lobbying_regulation_measure.txt:758
notes: Separate composite measure scored only for 2003, added to (not summed within) the 18-point index. Source: 'a thorough examination of the state codes' (line 753) — i.e., direct statute reading by Newmark, NOT BoS. Paper-defined and paper-coded, but the 1-4 scale lacks a transparent rubric mapping fines/sentences to scores. Considers gift-ban/prohibited-activity/disclosure violations classified as felony vs misdemeanor (endnote 7); also failure-to-file, failure-to-register, false statement penalties. Data missing for CO, TN, WV.
